task_completion.description: Verify that the Garmin BaseCamp application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Garmin BaseCamp main application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the waypoint creation or properties dialog is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Waypoint' or 'Waypoint Properties' dialog box is open over the main BaseCamp window.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct waypoint name is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Summit Camp' is typed into the Name field of the waypoint dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct coordinates are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The coordinates 42.4330 and -71.1155 (or equivalent formats like N 42.4330, W 71.1155) are typed into the Position/Coordinates field of the waypoint dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify that the waypoint is successfully created and visible in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waypoint 'Summit Camp' appears in the left-hand user data list pane or is visible as a marked point on the map interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to create the waypoint.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the mouse cursor interacting with BaseCamp menus, toolbars, and dialogs, rather than a terminal window running scripts to edit a GPX file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Garmin BaseCamp software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shown must be Garmin BaseCamp, not a text editor (like Notepad or VS Code) directly editing the fells_loop.gpx XML data.

integrity.description: Verify the waypoint was actively created during the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the waypoint dialog being filled out step-by-step, rather than the waypoint simply appearing instantly from a pre-loaded, modified file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on the fells_loop.gpx dataset. The requested waypoint coordinates (42.4330, -71.1155) are valid and fall within the bounding box of the dataset's existing points. Garmin BaseCamp may format the coordinates using cardinal directions (e.g., N 42.4330, W 71.1155); accept any standard coordinate representation that mathematically matches 42.4330 latitude and -71.1155 longitude.
